question_text:
Word Ladder

A transformation sequence from word beginWord to word endWord using a dictionary wordList is a sequence beginWord -> s1 -> s2 -> ... -> sk such that every adjacent pair differs by a single letter, and every si for 1 <= i <= k is in wordList. Note that beginWord does not need to be in wordList.

Return the number of words in the shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord, or 0 if no such sequence exists.

Constraints:

  • 1 <= beginWord.length <= 10
  • endWord.length == beginWord.length
  • 1 <= wordList.length <= 5000
  • All words are of the same length.
  • All words consist of lowercase English letters.
input_format: Strings beginWord and endWord, and a list wordList.
output_format: The length of the shortest transformation sequence, or 0 if none exists.
explanation:
BFS (Breadth-First Search)

Model as an unweighted graph where each word connects to its one-letter neighbors that are in wordList. Use BFS starting from beginWord. The first time endWord is reached gives the shortest path. Optimization: use a set for O(1) lookup and remove words once visited.

Time: O(M²×N) where M=word length, N=wordList size. Space: O(M²×N).
